name: Geography of India: Water Resources & Rivers in India

sourceRefs.label: 2025_q page 72 Q76 (Tamil Nadu rivers-tributaries mismatch, with answer key)
tnpscguru.in - Water Resources/Rivers TNPSC Q&A (11-question real past-paper set, strong TN focus)

researchNotes: One direct hit plus an 11-question web-sourced real past-paper set with a strong Tamil Nadu-specific focus (Cauvery/Coleroon split, Sivasamudram dam, Thamirabarani as TN's perennial river) — sensible given this is a Tamil Nadu state service exam, so TN-specific river facts are worth weighting heavily within this all-India topic.

reading.intro: Third sub-topic of Geography Unit II. Real exam evidence shows a strong Tamil-Nadu-specific lean even within the broader 'Rivers in India' clause — expect TN rivers/dams to come up disproportionately often for a TNPSC exam.

qa.question: Real exam: Which river-tributary pairing is INCORRECT — Cauvery-Amaravathi, Thamirabharani-Pampar, Vaigai-Pechiyar, Vellar-Manjalar?
qa.answer: Vaigai-Pechiyar AND Vellar-Manjalar are both incorrect pairings (marked '(3) and (4)' wrong in the real exam). Cauvery-Amaravathi and Thamirabharani-Pampar are correctly paired.

qa.question: Where does the river Kaveri (Cauvery) split into two channels — the Coleroon and the Kaveri?
Across which river was the Sivasamudram dam built?
Which is THE perennial river of Tamil Nadu (flows year-round, unlike the others which are seasonal)?
qa.answer: The Thamirabarani River (not Noyyal, Cauvery, or Vaigai — note Cauvery itself is largely seasonal/regulated within TN despite being a major river).

qa.question: What water-body type in Tamil Nadu has the most significant salinity problem?
qa.answer: Ground water (more so than river, canal, or pond water).

qa.question: In which state does the Jhelum river originate?
The Chambal river is a tributary of which major river?
What landform is typically found at the mouth of a FAST-flowing river (as opposed to a slow one)?
qa.answer: An Estuary (a slow river instead typically forms a Delta at its mouth, due to sediment deposition that a fast-flowing river's stronger current prevents from settling).

qa.question: Arrange these major rivers in North-to-South sequence: Godavari, Mahanadi, Krishna, Kaveri.
qa.answer: Mahanadi → Godavari → Krishna → Kaveri (north to south).

qa.question: Which of these rivers originate in Karnataka — Bhavani, Palar, Pennaiyar, Cauvery?
qa.answer: Pennaiyar, Cauvery, and Bhavani originate in Karnataka (Palar does not — it originates in Karnataka too in some classifications, but the real exam's marked answer excluded Palar from the Karnataka-origin set, including only options II, III, IV per that question's lettering — treat 'Cauvery and its Karnataka-origin tributaries' as the safe takeaway rather than memorizing the exact letter-option mapping).

qa.question: When was the Ganga Canal (Uttar Pradesh), one of India's first major irrigation works, opened?
